terms.example: On November 13, Pooja Sankar, CEO of Piazza, an online Q&A platform used by students and teachers, sat down with four women at Palantir to discuss their experiences working in technology.

high_scoring_sentences.text: Since then we’ve doubled in size every year while retaining our early-stage values: a startup culture, strong work ethic, and rigorous hiring standards.
At Palantir, we are only hiring the best and the brightest computer engineers to help us harness this awesome power to meet today’s vast analytical needs.
However it can no longer be denied that in today’s work environment, being able to deepen our understanding of an issue has made us all the more valuable.
We at Palantir Technologies believe that the next seismic shift already has begun.
Our products are built for real analysis with a focus on security, scalability, ease of use, and collaboration.
Palantir was built by technologists serious about protecting privacy and civil liberties.

As the CEO and founder of Palantir Technologies, I can say with pride that every engineer on the Palantir team is working with a sense of excitement and mission, convinced that we have created a product that will help analysts take quantum leaps in their knowledge base and their fields of particular expertise.
Thoughts on Palantir and the promise of analysis by our CEO, Alex Karp.

At Palantir, we’ve created a rich "conceptual environment," where each analyst can create his or her own visual intuitive space where they can absorb, understand, and interact with disparate information to come up with sophisticated findings.
Palantir Technologies is working to radically change how groups analyze information.

Thoughts on Palantir and the promise of analysis by our CEO, Alex Karp.
Palantir Technologies is working to radically change how groups analyze information.
From the technology we build to the problems we solve and organizations we support, our work is a reflection of who we are and what we believe.
We love to build things and solve hard problems, and we believe the best idea wins.
Our people are the makers and keepers of a unique culture that allows us to do serious work without taking ourselves too seriously.
We’re passionate about making a difference with mission-driven organizations through our philanthropic work.

Our work is inspired by what we believe .
At the heart of what we do is a desire to connect people with the information they need to achieve extraordinary outcomes.
From food to games to special events, life at Palantir is designed to energize and inspire.

Palantir is grounded in an engineering culture .
Perks, benefits, social activities, and learning opportunities: we believe people are our most important asset, so we invest in our people every day to promote productivity and reduce friction.
With a combination of perks, benefits, social activities, and learning opportunities, we invest every day in our most important asset: our people.
At Palantir, we work for the common good—within our organization and with other organizations around the world.
We’re building a future where data can be leveraged to serve people, create value, and improve quality of life.
Our environment is designed to make work energizing and to stimulate the best thinking.
While we certainly think we’re doing exciting, impactful work, we don’t want you to just take our word for it.
Focus on what matters: Palantir provides a variety of perks that help us focus on the mission by promoting productivity and freeing up time at and away from the office.
Find out more about us—we think you’ll like what you see.
We have a small gym and showers on-site, and provide organized yoga classes, on-site haircuts, a doctor, a chiropractor, massages, laundry and dry-cleaning.
You do have to speak up when things aren't right and build to fix what's broken.
Where you take the next step in your career matters.
Engineers build to solve problems, but at Palantir you don't have to be a computer scientist to be an engineer.
No sweat: We offer local gym memberships to all employees, and family members get a discounted rate.
Palantir interns apply their knowledge to real-world problems from day one.
We have organized games of pickup basketball, soccer, and ultimate Frisbee, and we field teams in local basketball leagues.
We have no set hours—as long as your work is getting done, you're free to work whatever schedule suits you.

We have a small gym and showers on-site, and provide organized yoga classes, on-site haircuts, a doctor, a chiropractor, massages, laundry, and dry cleaning.
No sweat: We offer a discounted rates for local gyms to all Palantirians and family members, as well as a wellness subsidy.
Palantir interns apply their knowledge to real-world problems from day one.
We have organized games of pickup basketball, soccer, and ultimate Frisbee, and we field teams in local basketball leagues.
We have no set hours—as long as your work is getting done, you're free to work whatever schedule suits you.
We believe in augmenting human intelligence, not replacing it.

At our headquarters in Palo Alto and at offices around the world we’ve assembled a team that combines practical expertise in distributed systems infrastructure, big data processing, user experience design, and data science.
We have always been, and continue to be, committed to helping organizations get value out of their data while protecting sensitive information from misuse and abuse.
Whatever the situation, our approach with all our clients is the same: to establish a partnership that transforms the way they use data in pursuit of their goals.

We’re focused on creating the world’s best user experience for working with data, one that empowers people to ask and answer complex questions without requiring them to master querying languages, statistical modeling, or the command line.
We are engineers on a mission.
We have proven products and an engineering mindset.
We saw a need for a different kind of technology, and we knew it would take a different kind of company to build it.
Whatever their role, each Palantirian combines an uncompromising engineering mindset with an unwavering focus on executing in service of the mission.

We are working to build a future in which public institutions, commercial enterprises, and non-profit organizations can use data to function as they were designed—to fulfill the mandates with which they’ve been entrusted, to deliver value to customers, and to distribute aid to those most in need.

That's why we build privacy-protective capabilities into our products, help customers understand how to use them responsibly, and work with advocacy groups and the policy community on how technology can be used to protect privacy interests today and in the future.
By establishing a true partnership, we help customers get the most out of our products and engineering expertise.
To achieve this, we build platforms for integrating, managing, and securing data on top of which we layer applications for fully interactive human-driven, machine-assisted analysis.
We seek out the most critical problems we can find—the ones that pose threats not only to many of the world’s most important institutions, but to the people they serve as well.
See the challenges we help our customers face

We send our engineers into the field to work directly with our customers—deploying our products, integrating their data, optimizing their workflows, and producing operational results in weeks, not years.
Organizations around the world are using Palantir to help them do their most important work.

We attract people who are intellectually diverse and contrarian at heart.
At our offices around the world, we’ve assembled a team that combines practical expertise in distributed systems infrastructure, big data processing, user experience design, and data science.
We have always been, and continue to be, committed to helping organizations get value out of their data while protecting sensitive information from misuse and abuse.
Whatever the situation, our approach with all our clients is the same: to establish a partnership that transforms the way they use data in pursuit of their goals.

We’re focused on creating the world’s best user experience for working with data, one that empowers people to ask and answer complex questions without requiring them to master querying languages, statistical modeling, or the command line.
We optimize for impact, not consensus.
We have proven products and an engineering mindset.
We saw a need for a different kind of technology, and we knew it would take a different kind of company to build it.
Whatever their role, each Palantirian combines an uncompromising engineering mindset with an unwavering focus on executing in service of the mission.
We build software that empowers organizations to effectively integrate their data, decisions, and operations.

We are working to build a future in which public institutions, commercial enterprises, and non-profit organizations can use data to function as they were designed—to fulfill the mandates with which they’ve been entrusted, to deliver value to customers, and to distribute aid to those most in need.
At Palantir, we believe that with good data and the right software, institutions can solve hard problems and change the world for the better.

That's why we build privacy-protective capabilities into our products, help customers understand how to use them responsibly, and work with advocacy groups and the policy community on how technology can be used to protect privacy interests today and in the future.
We built Palantir to ensure the future of the West, not to tinker at the margins.
To achieve this, we build platforms for integrating, managing, and securing data on top of which we layer applications for fully interactive human-driven, machine-assisted analysis.
We seek out the most critical problems we can find—the ones that pose threats not only to many of the world’s most important institutions, but to the people they serve as well.
Our platforms are used throughout the public, private, and non-profit sectors to help organizations quickly implement solutions to the hardest problems they face.
Sleek moving text that says "The world's hardest problems need the worlds smartest people."
Organizations around the world are using Palantir to help them do their most important work.

inclusion.example: On November 13, Pooja Sankar, CEO of Piazza, an online Q&A platform used by students and teachers, sat down with four women at Palantir to discuss their experiences working in technology.

civilizational.example: We built Palantir to ensure the future of the West, not to tinker at the margins.
